human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for live-event-production specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, pricing and refund boundary handling, safety and suspicious-ticket escalation posture, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to live-event permit, ticketing, guest, staffing, public-safety, throughput, and completion-evidence handling rather than generic hospitality or box-office support.

Authority sources cover official municipal permitting, insurance, and public-safety workflow plus FTC ticket-pricing and anti-bot guidance, CFPB payment-dispute guidance, and Event Safety Alliance standards and terminology references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, unsupported completion, ticketing and payment mismatch, refund and pricing exceptions, customer-impact safety and fraud escalation, high-volume festival flow, adjacent-lane routing, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/arts-entertainment-and-recreation/event-production-specialist/ so event-production execution remains distinct from guest services, front office, pricing and revenue, fraud and loss-prevention, and broader operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around pricing overrides, refund exceptions, suspicious-ticket handling, permit waivers, legal interpretation, and public-safety signoff authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated event production specialist for live-event readiness, ticketing and guest execution, throughput-aware event-day control, customer-impact escalation, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
